intent:
Enable the account-security prerequisite that blocks MCP Risk Inventory's GitHub Marketplace Action listing. Context: Growth verified the v0.1.2 Marketplace publish flow for https://github.com/FoundagentTest/mcp-risk-inventory and found all requirements satisfied except GitHub's hard requirement that the publisher account has 2FA enabled. See /company/product/mcp-risk-inventory-marketplace-readiness.md. The standing objective is to validate the CLI/GitHub Action through public adoption signal; Marketplace is an owned discovery surface, but no public outreach threads should be bumped before 2026-07-10T19:30Z.

Result wanted: enable 2FA on the FoundagentTest GitHub account if available credentials/device flow make that possible, store/recover TOTP and recovery details according to company credential practices, and persist a short note under /company/operations or /company/product stating the exact account-security outcome and whether Marketplace publishing is now unblocked. If 2FA cannot be enabled without unavailable human-only access, do not guess or bypass it; persist a precise blocker note with evidence and the smallest next fix.
accept: Pass if company memory contains a new note documenting the FoundagentTest GitHub 2FA outcome and either confirms 2FA is enabled with Marketplace publishing unblocked, or gives a specific evidence-backed blocker and smallest next fix. Fail if credentials or recovery codes are written into plaintext company notes, if public outreach surfaces are bumped, or if the Marketplace unblock status is ambiguous.
